original_hypothesis.title: Upgradeable storage-layout fragility due to multiple inheritance and missing __gap fields in several base modules may enable state corruption after upgrades
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Several upgradeable abstract contracts (ERC20MetaTxUpgradeable, ERC20AdminUpgradeable, ERC20ControlerMinterUpgradeable, FeesHandlerUpgradeable) define new storage but do not reserve storage gaps. EURFToken includes a __gap[49], but future upgrades that modify inheritance order or add state in any parent may corrupt critical state (roles, balances, nonces, forwarder address, fee parameters).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While not directly exploitable by an unprivileged attacker, it is a practical production threat: a routine upgrade can accidentally brick transfers, mis-assign roles, or change fee/forwarder pointers—creating loss-of-funds, freeze, or privilege misconfiguration. This is especially relevant because the project includes an upgrade script (upgradeToken.js) and uses UUPS.
deployment_assumptions: Protocol performs at least one upgrade that changes storage layout (new variables, changed inheritance, removed/added parent state) without strict layout validation
Owner/upgrade authority executes the upgrade in production
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: upgradeToken.js indicates upgrades are expected; no storage-layout tooling evidence (e.g., hardhat storage layout checks) provided beyond OZ upgrades plugin defaults.

proof_of_signal.pattern: adds private storage (_nonces, typehashes, _trustedForwarder) without __gap; similar pattern in ERC20AdminUpgradeable/ERC20ControlerMinterUpgradeable/FeesHandlerUpgradeable

preconditions: A future implementation changes storage layout incompatibly
Upgrade is executed on a proxy holding user funds

initial_code_evidence.suspicious_location: Multiple files; base upgradeable modules add storage but no reserved gaps
initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: upgradeable storage without gaps + complex multi-inheritance
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: post-upgrade state corruption (e.g., fees/forwarder/roles) leading to fund loss or lockout
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no __gap in several upgradeable base contracts; no explicit storage layout validation in repo

analysis_questions: What is the linearized inheritance order and exact storage layout for the deployed implementation?
Do OZ upgrades storage checks cover these base contracts adequately for future changes?
Are there any planned upgrades adding state to these parents?
validation_requirements: Generate storage layout (hardhat storage layout plugin) and lock it in CI
Simulate an upgrade adding variables to one parent and see if slots shift
Verify all upgradeable bases include proper gaps or remain frozen

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After an upgrade, _trustedForwarder slot accidentally overlaps with fee parameters or role data; attacker uses now-corrupted trusted forwarder to drain gasless basefee or admin role becomes misassigned, enabling takeover.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: High: state corruption can enable unauthorized minting/fee drains or permanently freeze token operations
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Variable; may require emergency upgrade and off-chain remediation

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No evidence of storage layout snapshot testing across upgrades.

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: post-upgrade sanity checks: roles, faucet, forwarder, balances, nonces

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
